title: 1.1: Adds `IsNullish` import from `go.k6.io/k6/js/common` in all 7 files where `IsNullish` is called (including `browser_context_mapping.go`, `helpers.go`, `element_handle_options.go`, `frame_options.go`, `layout.go`, `mouse_options.go`, `page_options.go`)

annotations.description: The js/common package must be imported in all 7 files where IsNullish is called: browser_context_mapping.go, browser/helpers.go, element_handle_options.go, frame_options.go, layout.go, mouse_options.go, page_options.go. Note that common/helpers.go only has function removal and requires no import since IsNullish is never called there.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch adds imports to exactly 7 files where IsNullish is called: browser_context_mapping.go (line 9), browser/helpers.go (line 39), element_handle_options.go (line 155), frame_options.go (line 284), layout.go (line 403), mouse_options.go (line 424), page_options.go (line 472).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states 'Do this by reusing existent utilities from js/common if possible'. [3: Logical Necessity] To use the IsNullish utility, the package must be imported in each file where it is used. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must import the js/common package in all files where IsNullish is called.

title: 4.1: Removes null/undefined verification checks without replacing them with equivalent functionality

annotations.description: Checks that null/undefined verification is preserved by replacing old checks with IsNullish, not simply removing them.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch replaces every sobekValueExists call and inline check with corresponding IsNullish call throughout all modified files (lines 17-29, 47-83, 93-104, 115-144, 163-276, 292-377, 410-413, 432-461, 480-500).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to 'eliminate duplicated verifications', not remove verification entirely. [3: Logical Necessity] The verification logic is needed for correct runtime behavior. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must replace, not remove, the verification checks.

title: 4.2: Modifies files outside the browser module scope, such as the `js/common` package implementation

annotations.description: Checks that the refactoring only affects the browser module and does not modify the existing js/common utility implementation.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch only modifies files within internal/js/modules/k6/browser/ directory (browser_context_mapping.go, helpers.go, page_mapping.go, route_mapping.go, element_handle_options.go, frame_options.go, common/helpers.go, layout.go, mouse_options.go, page_options.go), leaving js/common unchanged.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt specifically mentions 'the browser module' and 'reusing existent utilities from js/common'. [3: Logical Necessity] The js/common.IsNullish utility already exists and should be reused, not modified. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must not modify the js/common package implementation.
